mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction term shows zero marginal explanatory power in a multivariate regression over a 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes force the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal reverses direction during periods of stable inflation below 0.2% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly payrolls contractions signal immediate labor market stress and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of months following a payroll drop exceeding 100,000, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining retail sales confirms a broad-based demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of low GDPC1 growth and falling RSAFS produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression and accommodative financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 36-month rolling horizon.
